title: Production Evals For Agentic AI Systems - Nishant Gupta, Meta Superintelligence Labs
summary: Nishant Gupta from Meta Superintelligence Labs argues that traditional offline benchmarks and static datasets fail for autonomous AI systems in production. He presents architectural patterns for building continuous evaluation pipelines that measure tool use, planning, reasoning, drift detection, and operational reliability rather than model accuracy alone.
writeup: Nishant Gupta of Meta Superintelligence Labs argues that traditional offline benchmarks and static datasets break down for autonomous agents running in production. The talk presents architectural patterns for continuous evaluation pipelines that measure tool use, planning, reasoning, drift detection, and operational reliability instead of model accuracy alone. It also covers hallucination detection, collision detection, human-in-the-loop review, and observability as components of such a system. The core takeaway: evaluating agentic systems requires ongoing measurement of behavior and reliability, not one-time scoring against fixed test sets.
